title: Sarcocapnos integrifolia
intro: Sarcocapnos integrifolia, also known as the red catchfly or red poppy relative, is a flowering plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). The plant is rich in health benefits, including its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sarcocapnos integrifolia include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments to support wound healing and skin health.
taxonomy: Sarcocapnos integrifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. This plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. The genus Sarcocapnos is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as poppy flowers. Sarcocapnos integrifolia is a unique species within this genus, characterized by its distinctive leaves and bright yellow flowers. The classification of Sarcocapnos integrifolia is further supported by its inclusion in the Angiosperm Phylogeny Group's plant classification system.

constituents: Sarcocapnos integrifolia is known to contain various alkaloids as its active constituents.
